name: TAO 4h Deep Reset Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: a deeper pullback resets trend pressure, value is reclaimed, and continuation resumes from a cleaner location than shallow ema-touch entries
thesis: Cycle 76 includes trend reset continuation after a deeper pullback instead of shallow EMA-touch logic. TAO 4h is the best-fit lane because it is the only allowed lane with live trend-up contamination. That gives deeper reset continuation a real structural home instead of forcing it onto BTC or ETH chop.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h deep resets are still too sparse or reclaimed resets fail to continue after entry, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price pulls back deeply enough to reset positioning, reclaims value and structure, and then continues in the dominant direction.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In a trend-capable market, deeper pullbacks can clear weak hands and create better continuation location than shallow touch-and-go logic. The reclaim after reset is the structural proof.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it has the only meaningful trend-up bias in the basket, making deeper reset continuation far more plausible there than on ETH 4h chop.

indicator_role_map.ATR_14: reset depth threshold and risk sizing

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The reclaim may happen too late in the recovery, turning continuation entries into local exhaustion buys.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the setup trades but runners disappoint, take earlier partials before changing the reset depth logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower count than ETH mean-reversion ideas, but better asymmetry when trend resumes after reset.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears density and deep-reset continuation materially outperforms the prior failed-breakdown family.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or repeated reset-reclaim entries that fail before first target.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable because TAO trend contamination should create deeper resets

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit trend-capable lane for deeper reset continuation.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h shows enough reset-reclaim continuations with acceptable first-target expectancy.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h still undertrades or reset entries fail too quickly.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate count with a few larger trend-resumption legs.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same reset grammar survives on the nominal primary lane even if trend quality is lower.
test_lanes.continue_if: BTC 4h retains some reset-reclaim continuation with enough density.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h confirms the concept needs stronger trend contamination.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude, more mixed continuation than TAO.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the reset idea gains count without collapsing into noisy shallow pullbacks.
test_lanes.continue_if: BTC 1h increases count while preserving reset logic.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h turns the idea into the same weak intraday continuation noise we already killed.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Higher count, smaller reset continuations than TAO 4h.
